marketState: Neutral, tilted bearish on fundamentals - storage surplus and weak seasonality offset by hot near-term weather and oversold technicals
summary: Front-month NG is trading almost exactly at model fair value ($2.886 vs $2.917 fair, spread only -$0.03), so there is no strong mispricing to trade off currently. Seasonality is soft (price -15.3% YTD vs -3.4% median, 4-week odds of a higher price only 31%) but explicitly flagged Low reliability, so it shouldn't dominate. Weather is the standout bullish input - 1-7D demand is 16.2% above the 5-year norm sitting near the top of its range (rangePos 0.84), though 8-16D demand support is cooling (only +7.7%) and 24h demand momentum just turned negative. Storage remains the key bearish anchor: a +184 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average plus a sizeable +72 Bcf bearish build surprise argue against chasing strength. Technicals are bearish-trending on every timeframe (EMA50<200 everywhere) but multiple timeframes are stretched to the downside (1h/4h RSI 21-29, 1d price below the lower Bollinger band), which raises the odds of a near-term bounce even within the broader downtrend.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (low reliability, weak probHigher stats) and storage (+184 Bcf surplus, bearish surprise) argue bearish, while above-normal weather demand and a fair-value curve that rises toward $3.03 by +21D argue for eventual upside; with front price already sitting at 'FAIR' inside a wide $2.42-$3.42 band, there's no edge to lean either way yet. COT managed-money is net short but only at the 35th percentile, so positioning isn't extreme in either direction.
longTerm.trigger: Seasonal SHORT if price rallies into the upper fair-value band (~$3.10-$3.40) while the storage surplus persists/widens; seasonal LONG if price pulls back to $2.55-$2.65 (near lower fair-value band) or the storage surplus narrows/8-16D weather turns colder, positioning ahead of the winter contango already priced into Nov-Jan futures (Dec $3.77, Jan $4.23).

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D weather demand is 16.2% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, and 1h/4h RSI (21-29) plus a 1d close below the lower Bollinger band point to an oversold, stretched market ripe for a mean-reversion bounce toward fair value ($2.92-$2.95) even though the broader trend on every timeframe remains bearish (EMA50<200).
shortTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.74 (below near-term demand-driven support) or 8-16D weather demand rolling over further would confirm trend continuation rather than a bounce, invalidating the long.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise
1-7D weather demand +16.2% vs 5y, near top of range (rangePos 0.84) - supportive near-term
Seasonality weak and Low reliability; price already -15.3% YTD vs -3.4% median
Technicals bearish on all timeframes but 1h/4h RSI and 1d Bollinger show oversold stretch
Managed-money net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile) - not extreme
risks: 8-16D weather demand support is fading (+7.7% vs +16.2% for 1-7D) and 24h demand momentum just turned negative
Low seasonality reliability (50pp 10-90% spread) means the historical seasonal pattern carries little statistical edge right now
